# unifi_firewall_policy (Resource)

Manage a UniFi firewall policy.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manage a host-scoped service allow policy between source and destination firewall zones.
data "unifi_site" "main" {
  name = "Default"
}

resource "unifi_network" "trusted" {
  site_id                 = data.unifi_site.main.id
  management              = "GATEWAY"
  name                    = "trusted"
  enabled                 = true
  vlan_id                 = 20
  isolation_enabled       = false
  cellular_backup_enabled = false
  internet_access_enabled = true
  mdns_forwarding_enabled = true

  ipv4_configuration = {
    auto_scale_enabled = false
    host_ip_address    = "10.20.0.1"
    prefix_length      = 24
  }
}

resource "unifi_network" "services" {
  site_id                 = data.unifi_site.main.id
  management              = "GATEWAY"
  name                    = "services"
  enabled                 = true
  vlan_id                 = 30
  isolation_enabled       = true
  cellular_backup_enabled = false
  internet_access_enabled = true
  mdns_forwarding_enabled = false

  ipv4_configuration = {
    auto_scale_enabled = false
    host_ip_address    = "10.30.0.1"
    prefix_length      = 24
  }
}

resource "unifi_firewall_zone" "trusted" {
  site_id     = data.unifi_site.main.id
  name        = "trusted"
  network_ids = [unifi_network.trusted.id]
}

resource "unifi_firewall_zone" "services" {
  site_id     = data.unifi_site.main.id
  name        = "services"
  network_ids = [unifi_network.services.id]
}

resource "unifi_firewall_policy" "trusted_to_services" {
  site_id              = data.unifi_site.main.id
  enabled              = true
  name                 = "trusted-to-services-host"
  action               = "ALLOW"
  allow_return_traffic = false
  source_zone_id       = unifi_firewall_zone.trusted.id
  source_filter = {
    type                   = "NETWORK"
    network_ids            = [unifi_network.trusted.id]
    network_match_opposite = false
  }
  destination_zone_id = unifi_firewall_zone.services.id
  destination_filter = {
    type                      = "IP_ADDRESS"
    ip_addresses              = ["10.30.0.20"]
    ip_address_match_opposite = false
    port_filter = {
      type           = "PORTS"
      match_opposite = false
      ports          = ["53", "80", "443"]
    }
  }
  ip_version = "IPV4"
  protocol_filter = {
    type        = "PRESET"
    preset_name = "TCP_UDP"
  }
  logging_enabled = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `action` (String) Policy action. Supported values: `ALLOW`, `BLOCK`, `REJECT`.
- `destination_zone_id` (String)
- `enabled` (Boolean)
- `ip_version` (String) IP version scope. Supported values: `IPV4`, `IPV6`, `IPV4_AND_IPV6`.
- `logging_enabled` (Boolean)
- `name` (String)
- `site_id` (String) Site UUID that owns the firewall policy.
- `source_zone_id` (String)

### Optional

- `allow_return_traffic` (Boolean) Required when `action` is `ALLOW`. Creates the derived reverse policy to allow return traffic. Set this explicitly to `true` or `false` because current UniFi controller builds may reject `ALLOW` rules when the field is omitted.
- `connection_state_filter` (Set of String) Optional connection state filter values: `NEW`, `INVALID`, `ESTABLISHED`, `RELATED`.
- `description` (String)
- `destination_filter` (Attributes) Optional destination traffic filter. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--destination_filter))
- `ipsec_filter` (String) Optional IPsec traffic filter. Supported values: `MATCH_ENCRYPTED`, `MATCH_NOT_ENCRYPTED`.
- `protocol_filter` (Attributes) Optional protocol filter nested under `ip_version`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--protocol_filter))
- `schedule` (Attributes) Optional schedule configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--schedule))
- `source_filter` (Attributes) Optional source traffic filter. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source_filter))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `index` (Number) Controller-assigned firewall policy ordering index.

<a id="nestedatt--destination_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `destination_filter`

Required:

- `type` (String) Supported values: `PORT`, `NETWORK`, `IP_ADDRESS`, `IPV6_IID`, `REGION`, `VPN_SERVER`, `SITE_TO_SITE_VPN_TUNNEL`, `DOMAIN`, `APPLICATION`, `APPLICATION_CATEGORY`.

Optional:

- `application_category_ids` (Set of Number) DPI category IDs for destination `APPLICATION_CATEGORY` filters.
- `application_ids` (Set of Number) DPI application IDs for destination `APPLICATION` filters.
- `domains` (Set of String) Domain list for destination `DOMAIN` filters.
- `ip_address_match_opposite` (Boolean)
- `ip_address_traffic_matching_list_id` (String) Traffic matching list UUID for `IP_ADDRESS` filters.
- `ip_addresses` (Set of String) IP addresses, CIDR subnets, or address ranges for `IP_ADDRESS` filters.
- `ipv6_iid` (String) IPv6 IID filter value for `IPV6_IID` filters.
- `ipv6_iid_match_opposite` (Boolean)
- `mac_address` (String) Additional source MAC address selector. Only valid for source `NETWORK`, `IP_ADDRESS`, and `IPV6_IID` filters.
- `mac_addresses` (Set of String) MAC address set for `MAC_ADDRESS` source filters.
- `network_ids` (Set of String)
- `network_match_opposite` (Boolean)
- `port_filter` (Attributes) Optional nested port filter. `PORT` endpoint filters require this block. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--destination_filter--port_filter))
- `region_codes` (Set of String) ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes for `REGION` filters.
- `site_to_site_vpn_tunnel_id` (String) Site-to-site VPN tunnel UUID for `SITE_TO_SITE_VPN_TUNNEL` filters.
- `vpn_server_ids` (Set of String) VPN server UUIDs for `VPN_SERVER` filters.
- `vpn_server_match_opposite` (Boolean)

<a id="nestedatt--destination_filter--port_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `destination_filter.port_filter`

Required:

- `type` (String) Supported values: `PORTS`, `TRAFFIC_MATCHING_LIST`.

Optional:

- `match_opposite` (Boolean)
- `ports` (Set of String)
- `traffic_matching_list_id` (String)



<a id="nestedatt--protocol_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `protocol_filter`

Required:

- `type` (String) Supported values: `NAMED_PROTOCOL`, `PROTOCOL_NUMBER`, `PRESET`.

Optional:

- `match_opposite` (Boolean) When true, match all protocols except the selected one. Used by `NAMED_PROTOCOL` and `PROTOCOL_NUMBER`.
- `named_protocol` (String) Named protocol when `type` is `NAMED_PROTOCOL`. Current UniFi controller builds reliably accept `ICMP` here. For TCP/UDP service rules, prefer `type = PRESET` with `preset_name = "TCP_UDP"` plus a nested destination `port_filter`.
- `preset_name` (String) Preset name when `type` is `PRESET`. Current controller preset is `TCP_UDP`.
- `protocol_number` (Number) IANA protocol number when `type` is `PROTOCOL_NUMBER`.


<a id="nestedatt--schedule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule`

Required:

- `mode` (String) Supported values: `EVERY_DAY`, `EVERY_WEEK`, `ONE_TIME_ONLY`, `CUSTOM`.

Optional:

- `date` (String) Date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format for `ONE_TIME_ONLY` mode.
- `repeat_on_days` (Set of String) Days used by `EVERY_WEEK` and `CUSTOM` modes.
- `start_date` (String) Start date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format for `CUSTOM` mode.
- `start_time` (String) Start time in `HH:MM` format.
- `stop_date` (String) Stop date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format for `CUSTOM` mode.
- `stop_time` (String) Stop time in `HH:MM` format.


<a id="nestedatt--source_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source_filter`

Required:

- `type` (String) Supported values: `PORT`, `NETWORK`, `MAC_ADDRESS`, `IP_ADDRESS`, `IPV6_IID`, `REGION`, `VPN_SERVER`, `SITE_TO_SITE_VPN_TUNNEL`.

Optional:

- `application_category_ids` (Set of Number) DPI category IDs for destination `APPLICATION_CATEGORY` filters.
- `application_ids` (Set of Number) DPI application IDs for destination `APPLICATION` filters.
- `domains` (Set of String) Domain list for destination `DOMAIN` filters.
- `ip_address_match_opposite` (Boolean)
- `ip_address_traffic_matching_list_id` (String) Traffic matching list UUID for `IP_ADDRESS` filters.
- `ip_addresses` (Set of String) IP addresses, CIDR subnets, or address ranges for `IP_ADDRESS` filters.
- `ipv6_iid` (String) IPv6 IID filter value for `IPV6_IID` filters.
- `ipv6_iid_match_opposite` (Boolean)
- `mac_address` (String) Additional source MAC address selector. Only valid for source `NETWORK`, `IP_ADDRESS`, and `IPV6_IID` filters.
- `mac_addresses` (Set of String) MAC address set for `MAC_ADDRESS` source filters.
- `network_ids` (Set of String)
- `network_match_opposite` (Boolean)
- `port_filter` (Attributes) Optional nested port filter. `PORT` endpoint filters require this block. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source_filter--port_filter))
- `region_codes` (Set of String) ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes for `REGION` filters.
- `site_to_site_vpn_tunnel_id` (String) Site-to-site VPN tunnel UUID for `SITE_TO_SITE_VPN_TUNNEL` filters.
- `vpn_server_ids` (Set of String) VPN server UUIDs for `VPN_SERVER` filters.
- `vpn_server_match_opposite` (Boolean)

<a id="nestedatt--source_filter--port_filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source_filter.port_filter`

Required:

- `type` (String) Supported values: `PORTS`, `TRAFFIC_MATCHING_LIST`.

Optional:

- `match_opposite` (Boolean)
- `ports` (Set of String)
- `traffic_matching_list_id` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import a site-scoped firewall policy by site and policy identifier.
terraform import unifi_firewall_policy.trusted_to_iot <site_id>/<firewall_policy_id>
```
